source.notes: Imported from Azure DevOps work item 6445

observedSteps: User Story

As a guest user, I want the Home page to display guest-state controls and guest-accessible feature buttons, so that I can use the application before login and open the Login page from the Person/Profile button.
Application Context
WashTab is a single page application (SPA). The workflow begins at:
http://localhost/washtabui/home

The Home page is the shared landing page for both unauthenticated and authenticated users. This workflow validates only the guest-state behavior of the existing Home page before login. The guest state is not a separate page from the authenticated Home state.
Entry Conditions
The application is launched at http://localhost/washtabui/home
The user is unauthenticated and has not signed in
The Home page loads in guest state
Workflow Scope
This workflow validates:
Initial Home page load in guest state
Visibility and interactivity of approved guest-state controls
Visibility and interactivity of shared Home shell controls
Navigation from the Person/Profile button to the Login page or Login view
Navigation from guest-accessible feature buttons to their supported destinations
Absence or non-actionability of authenticated-only controls and feature buttons
Absence of the logged-in user name button while unauthenticated
Primary Navigation Journey
Launch the application at http://localhost/washtabui/home
Verify the Home page loads in guest state
Verify the Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, and approved guest-accessible feature buttons are visible and enabled
Select the Person/Profile button and verify navigation to the Login page or Login view
Return to the Home page guest state as needed
Select the Customer signup button and verify navigation to the customer-search page
Select the Clean survey button and verify navigation to the vehicle-survey page
Select the Site survey button and verify navigation to the survey page
Verify authenticated-only controls and the logged-in user name button are not visible or not actionable while in guest state
Approved Test Data
Application entry URL: http://localhost/washtabui/home
No additional approved credentials or user data were provided
Guest-state execution should remain unauthenticated throughout the workflow
Page / Component Elements
Home Page Guest-State Elements:
Person/Profile button
Customer signup button
Clean survey button
Site survey button
Shared Home Shell Controls:
Back button
Notification button
Home button
Navigation Destinations:
Login page or Login view
Customer-search page
Vehicle-survey page
Survey page
Ownership Guidance:
All guest-state Home controls belong to the Home page POM/resource
Shared Home shell controls belong to the Home page POM/resource
The Person/Profile button belongs to the Home page POM/resource
The Login page must not own the Person/Profile button because navigation originates from Home
Business Rules
When an unauthenticated user launches the application, the Home page must load in guest state
Guest-state controls and approved guest-accessible feature buttons must be visible and enabled
Selecting the Person/Profile button must open the Login page or Login view
Guest-accessible feature buttons must navigate to their supported destinations
Authenticated-only controls and authenticated-only feature buttons must not be visible or must not be actionable in guest state
The logged-in user name button must not be visible while the user is unauthenticated
The Home page guest state and authenticated state are different states of the same Home page resource
Validation Expectations
Validate successful Home page load in guest state
Validate visibility and enabled state of:
Validate navigation from:
Person/Profile button to Login page or Login view
Customer signup button to customer-search page
Clean survey button to vehicle-survey page
Site survey button to survey page
Validate authenticated-only controls and feature buttons are absent or non-actionable in guest state
Validate the logged-in user name button is not visible in guest state
Transition Expectations
Launching the application while unauthenticated transitions the application into Home page guest state
Clicking the Person/Profile button transitions from Home guest state to the Login page or Login view
Clicking Customer signup transitions from Home guest state to the customer-search page
Clicking Clean survey transitions from Home guest state to the vehicle-survey page
Clicking Site survey transitions from Home guest state to the survey page
While remaining unauthenticated, authenticated-only controls must not become accessible
Resource Reuse Guidance
Establish the Home page as the foundational reusable Home page resource/POM
Reuse the Home page resource across future guest-state and authenticated-state workflows
Reuse shared Home shell controls from the Home page resource instead of duplicating ownership in downstream resources
Reuse the Person/Profile button from the Home page resource for Login navigation workflows
Avoid assigning ownership of the Person/Profile button to the Login page resource
Downstream Automation Guidance
Model the Home page as a single reusable resource supporting multiple application states
Keep guest-state and authenticated-state validations within the same Home page resource structure
Use the approved application entry URL consistently across manual and automation assets
Keep navigation ownership aligned with the originating page resource
Reuse shared Home shell controls across downstream Home-related workflows
Generate navigation validations for all approved guest-accessible feature buttons
Generate negative validations for authenticated-only control absence and non-actionability in guest state
Acceptance Criteria
Given the user launches the application and navigates to http://localhost/washtabui/home, when the page loads, then the Home page should load successfully in guest state

Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then the Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, Customer signup button, Clean survey button, and Site survey button should be visible and enabled
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks the Person/Profile button, then the Login page or Login view should open
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks the Customer signup button, then the customer-search page should open
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks the Clean survey button, then the vehicle-survey page should open
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks the Site survey button, then the survey page should open

Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then authenticated-only controls and authenticated-only feature buttons should not be visible or should not be actionable
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then the logged-in user name button should not be visible

externalContext.title: Home Feature - Guest State
externalContext.description:
1. User Story
 
 As a guest user, I want the Home page to display guest-state controls and guest-accessible feature buttons, so that I can use the application before login and open the Login page from the Person/Profile button.
 
 2. Application Context
 
 WashTab is a single page application (SPA). The workflow begins at:
 http://localhost/washtabui/home
 
 The Home page is the shared landing page for both unauthenticated and authenticated users. This workflow validates only the guest-state behavior of the existing Home page before login. The guest state is not a separate page from the authenticated Home state.
 
 3. Entry Conditions
 
 - The application is launched at http://localhost/washtabui/home
 - The user is unauthenticated and has not signed in
 - The Home page loads in guest state
 
 4. Workflow Scope
 
 This workflow validates:
 - Initial Home page load in guest state
 - Visibility and interactivity of approved guest-state controls
 - Visibility and interactivity of shared Home shell controls
 - Navigation from the Person/Profile button to the Login page or Login view
 - Navigation from guest-accessible feature buttons to their supported destinations
 - Absence or non-actionability of authenticated-only controls and feature buttons
 - Absence of the logged-in user name button while unauthenticated
 
 5. Primary Navigation Journey
 
 - Launch the application at http://localhost/washtabui/home
 - Verify the Home page loads in guest state
 - Verify the Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, and approved guest-accessible feature buttons are visible and enabled
 - Select the Person/Profile button and verify navigation to the Login page or Login view
 - Return to the Home page guest state as needed
 - Select the Customer signup button and verify navigation to the customer-search page
 - Return to the Home page guest state as needed
 - Select the Clean survey button and verify navigation to the vehicle-survey page
 - Return to the Home page guest state as needed
 - Select the Site survey button and verify navigation to the survey page
 - Verify authenticated-only controls and the logged-in user name button are not visible or not actionable while in guest state
 
 6. Approved Test Data
 
 - Application entry URL: http://localhost/washtabui/home
 - No additional approved credentials or user data were provided
 - Guest-state execution should remain unauthenticated throughout the workflow
 
 7. Page / Component Elements
 
 Home Page Guest-State Elements:
 - Person/Profile button
 - Customer signup button
 - Clean survey button
 - Site survey button
 
 Shared Home Shell Controls:
 - Back button
 - Notification button
 - Home button
 
 Navigation Destinations:
 - Login page or Login view
 - Customer-search page
 - Vehicle-survey page
 - Survey page
 
 Ownership Guidance:
 - All guest-state Home controls belong to the Home page POM/resource
 - Shared Home shell controls belong to the Home page POM/resource
 - The Person/Profile button belongs to the Home page POM/resource
 - The Login page must not own the Person/Profile button because navigation originates from Home
 
 8. Business Rules
 
 - When an unauthenticated user launches the application, the Home page must load in guest state
 - Guest-state controls and approved guest-accessible feature buttons must be visible and enabled
 - Selecting the Person/Profile button must open the Login page or Login view
 - Guest-accessible feature buttons must navigate to their supported destinations
 - Authenticated-only controls and authenticated-only feature buttons must not be visible or must not be actionable in guest state
 - The logged-in user name button must not be visible while the user is unauthenticated
 - The Home page guest state and authenticated state are different states of the same Home page resource
 
 9. Validation Expectations
 
 - Validate successful Home page load in guest state
 - Validate visibility and enabled state of:
 - Person/Profile button
 - Back button
 - Notification button
 - Home button
 - Customer signup button
 - Clean survey button
 - Site survey button
 - Validate navigation from:
 - Person/Profile button to Login page or Login view
 - Customer signup button to customer-search page
 - Clean survey button to vehicle-survey page
 - Site survey button to survey page
 - Validate authenticated-only controls and feature buttons are absent or non-actionable in guest state
 - Validate the logged-in user name button is not visible in guest state
 
 10. Transition Expectations
 
 - Launching the application while unauthenticated transitions the application into Home page guest state
 - Clicking the Person/Profile button transitions from Home guest state to the Login page or Login view
 - Clicking Customer signup transitions from Home guest state to the customer-search page
 - Clicking Clean survey transitions from Home guest state to the vehicle-survey page
 - Clicking Site survey transitions from Home guest state to the survey page
 - While remaining unauthenticated, authenticated-only controls must not become accessible
 
 11. Resource Reuse Guidance
 
 - Establish the Home page as the foundational reusable Home page resource/POM
 - Reuse the Home page resource across future guest-state and authenticated-state workflows
 - Reuse shared Home shell controls from the Home page resource instead of duplicating ownership in downstream resources
 - Reuse the Person/Profile button from the Home page resource for Login navigation workflows
 - Avoid assigning ownership of the Person/Profile button to the Login page resource
 
 12. Downstream Automation Guidance
 
 - Model the Home page as a single reusable resource supporting multiple application states
 - Keep guest-state and authenticated-state validations within the same Home page resource structure
 - Use the approved application entry URL consistently across manual and automation assets
 - Keep navigation ownership aligned with the originating page resource
 - Reuse shared Home shell controls across downstream Home-related workflows
 - Generate navigation validations for all approved guest-accessible feature buttons
 - Generate negative validations for authenticated-only control absence and non-actionability in guest state
 
 13. Acceptance Criteria
 
 - Given the user launches the application and navigates to http://localhost/washtabui/home, when the page loads, then the Home page should load successfully in guest state
 - Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then the Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, Customer signup button, Clean survey button, and Site survey button should be visible and enabled
 - Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks the Person/Profile button, then the Login page or Login view should open
 - Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks the Customer signup button, then the customer-search page should open
 - Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks the Clean survey button, then the vehicle-survey page should open
 - Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks the Site survey button, then the survey page should open
 - Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then authenticated-only controls and authenticated-only feature buttons should not be visible or should not be actionable
 - Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then the logged-in user name button should not be visible
